description: Selector mapping from broken CSS selectors to healed semantic locators after UI redesign
strategy: Replace all CSS-based selectors with semantic Playwright locators (getByRole, getByText, getByAltText) that are resilient to design changes

mappings.new_selector: page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'To get started, edit the page.tsx file.' })
mappings.reason: CSS class .hero-title no longer exists after redesign. getByRole('heading') is resilient to class name changes.
CSS class .logo no longer exists. Alt text is stable across redesigns.

mappings.new_selector: page.getByText('Looking for a starting point or more instructions?')
mappings.reason: CSS class .subtitle no longer exists. Text content is the most reliable locator.
ID #deploy-btn no longer exists. getByRole('link') with name is semantic and resilient.
CSS class .docs-link no longer exists. Role-based locator is stable.
CSS class .templates no longer exists. Role-based locator is stable.
CSS class .learn-link no longer exists. Role-based locator is stable.
CSS class .deploy-icon no longer exists. Alt text is stable.
